note: Hand-curated capabilities manifest for the Ask GTFS·X assistant (issue #68). Source of truth for the assistant's click-paths, plan gates, and honest not-supported list. When you add/rename a panel, tool, or guided fix, update this file (PR checklist). deepLink.sidebarSection ids must be real SidebarSection values and deepLink.bottomTab ids real BottomPanelTab values (validated by src/assistant/__tests__/manifest.test.ts); every docs url must exist in the docs search index; every plan is 'all' or a real FeatureKey.

capabilities.name: Agency setup
capabilities.purpose: Enter the agency-level fields every feed needs: agency_name, agency_url, agency_timezone, plus agency_id and NTD ID for FTA reporting.
capabilities.whenToUse: First thing on a new feed, or when agency details, timezone, or NTD ID need fixing.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Agency.

capabilities.name: Service calendars
capabilities.purpose: Define service patterns (which days service runs) in calendar.txt and calendar_dates.txt, including holiday exceptions.
capabilities.whenToUse: Setting weekday/weekend/holiday service, or adding a US-holiday exception day.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Calendars. Exceptions live on the Exceptions tab of a calendar (with an 'Add US holidays' picker).

capabilities.name: Fares (v1 and v2 Areas)
capabilities.purpose: Author fare_attributes/fare_rules (Fares v1), and — with Fares v2 enabled — the Areas editor (areas.txt + stop_areas.txt). Transfers are edited here too.
capabilities.whenToUse: Adding fares to a feed, or defining fare areas.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Fares. Fares v2 tabs appear once you enable 'Fares v2' in Settings.
capabilities.limitations: Fares v2 in-app currently ships the Areas editor only; the rest of the v2 model (fare products, leg/transfer rules, rider categories) is not fully editable yet, though imported v2 files are preserved on export.

capabilities.name: Routes & shapes
capabilities.purpose: Create and edit routes (route_type, names, colors), draw route geometry on the map, and manage each route's shapes/patterns.
capabilities.whenToUse: Adding or editing a route, drawing a route line, or setting route type/color.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Routes. Open a route to reach its Details / Stops / Trips / Shapes tabs.

capabilities.name: Stops
capabilities.purpose: Place, name, and edit stops on the map; set wheelchair_boarding and other stop fields; filter the stop list.
capabilities.whenToUse: Adding stops, renaming, fixing accessibility flags, or finding a specific stop.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Stops. Use 'Place Stops on Map' to add; stop names auto-suggest from the nearest intersection.

capabilities.name: Stations, levels & pathways
capabilities.purpose: Model multi-level stations with levels.txt and pathways.txt (walkways, stairs, elevators between platforms).
capabilities.whenToUse: Building a station complex with pathways/levels.
capabilities.clickPath: Enable 'Stations & pathways' in Settings, then Left rail → Stations.
capabilities.limitations: The Stations nav item appears only when the 'Stations & pathways' feature is enabled in Settings (or the feed already contains levels/pathways).

capabilities.name: Frequency-based service
capabilities.purpose: Author headway-based service (e.g. 'a bus every 15 minutes') in frequencies.txt instead of explicit per-trip times.
capabilities.whenToUse: Modeling turn-up-and-go service by headway rather than a fixed timetable.
capabilities.clickPath: Enable 'Frequency-based service' in Settings, then Left rail → Frequencies.
capabilities.limitations: The Frequencies nav item appears only when the 'Frequency-based service' feature is enabled in Settings (or the feed already has frequencies.txt).

capabilities.name: Blocks (vehicle interlining)
capabilities.purpose: Group the trips a single vehicle runs in sequence (block_id) and visualize/validate them on a Gantt, including the Quick Block auto-blocker.
capabilities.whenToUse: Assigning trips to vehicle blocks or checking for block overlaps.
capabilities.clickPath: Enable 'Blocks' in Settings, then Left rail → Blocks.
capabilities.limitations: The Blocks nav item stays hidden until you enable 'Blocks' in Settings, even if the feed already carries block_id.

capabilities.name: Flex Zones (GTFS-Flex) & booking rules
capabilities.purpose: Author demand-response / paratransit service: draw GTFS-Flex zones (locations.geojson) and attach booking_rules.txt.
capabilities.whenToUse: Modeling dial-a-ride, microtransit, deviated-route, or paratransit service.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Flex Zones (on by default; toggle under 'Demand response' in Settings).

capabilities.name: Cost estimation
capabilities.purpose: Estimate operating cost from service levels; system-level summary is free, per-route breakdown is on the Planner suite.
capabilities.whenToUse: Estimating what a service plan costs to operate.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Costs.
capabilities.limitations: System-level cost is free; the per-route breakdown requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey analysis_basic).

capabilities.name: Stop analysis
capabilities.purpose: Analyze stop spacing/balancing, trips-per-day intensity, and accessibility gaps, with map overlays.
capabilities.whenToUse: Checking stop balance, service intensity by stop, or accessibility gaps.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Stop Analysis.
capabilities.limitations: Accessibility gaps hand off to the Validation 'fill wheelchair_boarding' fix rather than editing stops directly.

capabilities.name: Demographic coverage
capabilities.purpose: Measure population and jobs within walking distance of service using census data; per-route breakdown on Planner.
capabilities.whenToUse: Reporting who your service reaches, or comparing coverage across routes.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Coverage.
capabilities.limitations: System-level coverage is free; the per-route breakdown requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey analysis_basic).

capabilities.name: Transit access isochrones
capabilities.purpose: Drop a pin and see where a rider can reach on your network in 15/30/45 minutes (walk + wait + in-vehicle), with population, jobs, and equity populations in each contour.
capabilities.whenToUse: Showing schedule-based travel-time reach from a point.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Access, then drop a pin on the map.
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey access_isochrones).

capabilities.name: Title VI equity analysis
capabilities.purpose: Generate FTA-aligned Title VI reports comparing service levels across minority and low-income populations.
capabilities.whenToUse: Preparing a Title VI service-equity analysis.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Title VI.
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey analysis_title_vi).

capabilities.name: Service Alerts (GTFS-Realtime)
capabilities.purpose: Author GTFS-Realtime Service Alerts (detours, delays, stop closures) published to a live feed apps consume, without republishing the schedule.
capabilities.whenToUse: Posting a detour, delay, or stop-closure alert on a published feed.
capabilities.clickPath: Enable 'Service Alerts' in Settings (on by default once the feed is published), then Left rail → Service Alerts.
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey service_alerts). Only Service Alerts are authored in-app — not TripUpdates or VehiclePositions.

capabilities.name: Feed variants
capabilities.purpose: Fork a feed into named service alternatives, compare cost and service metrics side by side, and export any variant as a standalone GTFS feed.
capabilities.whenToUse: Comparing service scenarios (A vs B) or exporting an alternative.
capabilities.clickPath: Top bar → variants dropdown → 'Manage variants…' (variants have no left-rail item).
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey variants). Opened from the top-bar variants dropdown, not the left rail.

capabilities.name: Feature settings
capabilities.purpose: Turn advanced GTFS features on/off per feed (Stations & pathways, Frequencies, Blocks, Demand response, Service Alerts, Fares v2, Flag-stop service) so the editor stays lean.
capabilities.whenToUse: Revealing an advanced feature's panel/controls, or hiding ones you don't use.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Settings.

capabilities.name: Transfers
capabilities.purpose: Author transfers.txt — transfer rules between routes/stops (timed connections, minimum transfer times).
capabilities.whenToUse: Defining timed connections or minimum transfer times.
capabilities.clickPath: Enable 'Transfers' in Settings, then edit them from the Fares panel (Transfers tab).
capabilities.limitations: Transfers have no standalone nav section — they're a tab inside the Fares panel, revealed by the Transfers feature toggle.

capabilities.name: Timetables & trips editor
capabilities.purpose: Edit trips and stop_times in a spreadsheet-style grid: per-cell times, arrival/departure split, skip/interpolate/timed stop states, opposite-direction companion view.
capabilities.whenToUse: Entering or editing schedule times, adding trips, or reading inbound/outbound side by side.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Timetable tab (pick a route + service from the toolbar).

capabilities.name: Validation
capabilities.purpose: Continuously validate the feed against the GTFS spec and surface errors/warnings, many with one-click fixes.
capabilities.whenToUse: Diagnosing why a feed is failing validation, or clearing spec errors before export.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Validation tab.

capabilities.name: Service summary
capabilities.purpose: See a rolled-up summary of the feed's service — trips, spans, and service levels — for a sanity check.
capabilities.whenToUse: Getting a quick overview of what service the feed describes.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Service Summary tab.

capabilities.name: Named snapshots
capabilities.purpose: Keep a history of named snapshots of the feed and restore any prior state.
capabilities.whenToUse: Saving a checkpoint before a big edit, or rolling back.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Snapshots tab.
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey snapshot_history).

capabilities.name: Hosted publishing
capabilities.purpose: Publish the feed to a stable public URL riders, regulators, and the Mobility Database can rely on, with a rider mini-site and embeds.
capabilities.whenToUse: Turning a feed into a hosted, shareable GTFS URL.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Publish tab.
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey managed_publishing).

capabilities.name: Rider mini-site & embed widgets
capabilities.purpose: Generate copy-paste embed snippets (schedules, route maps, stop times) and a rider mini-site for a published feed.
capabilities.whenToUse: Putting schedules/maps on an agency website.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Embed tab (feed must be published).
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey embeds).

capabilities.name: One-click validation fixes
capabilities.purpose: Auto-fix common validation issues: fill blank first/last-stop times, bulk-fill missing wheelchair_boarding, remove orphan/ghost trips, delete unused stops, clear a route_desc that only repeats the name.
capabilities.whenToUse: A validation message shows a 'Fix' button — clearing a class of errors quickly.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Validation tab → 'Fix' on a message (or 'Fix all N of this type').
capabilities.limitations: Only issues with a registered fix show a Fix button; others are manual.

capabilities.name: Generate shapes from stops
capabilities.purpose: Create shapes.txt geometry for a feed that has stops but no shapes, routing through the stops (snapped to roads or straight lines).
capabilities.whenToUse: A feed imports without shapes and you want route lines on the map / in the export.
capabilities.clickPath: Post-import success screen → 'Generate shapes from stops', or Bottom panel → Validation tab → the 'Generate shapes' fix. Both open the Shapes-from-Stops dialog (Snap to roads vs straight lines).
capabilities.limitations: Uses Mapbox Directions through stops as waypoints; quality depends on the stop sequence. Snap-to-roads needs road-network coverage.

capabilities.name: Snap drawn routes to roads
capabilities.purpose: Snap a hand-drawn route line to the road network (Mapbox Map Matching) so shapes follow streets.
capabilities.whenToUse: Drawing a route and wanting it to hug the roads.
capabilities.clickPath: 'Snap to road' is a global setting (on by default); it applies when you finish drawing a route on the map.
capabilities.limitations: If the match looks wrong you can keep the raw drawn line instead.

capabilities.name: Simplify shape
capabilities.purpose: Reduce the number of points in a shape (Ramer–Douglas–Peucker) for a lighter, cleaner geometry, with a live preview.
capabilities.whenToUse: A shape has too many vertices and you want to thin it.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Routes → open a route → Shapes tab → 'Simplify' on a shape (pick a level).

capabilities.name: Generate trips
capabilities.purpose: Generate a set of trips for a route/pattern from either explicit start times or a frequency/headway, laying out stop_times automatically.
capabilities.whenToUse: Building a timetable from scratch for a route.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Timetable tab → toolbar → 'Generate trips…'.

capabilities.name: Set run time
capabilities.purpose: Re-lay every trip on a pattern to a new end-to-end run time (start times fixed, intermediate stops re-interpolated).
capabilities.whenToUse: Adjusting how long the route takes end to end across all its trips.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Timetable tab → toolbar → 'Set run time…'.

capabilities.name: Estimate travel times
capabilities.purpose: Estimate stop-to-stop times by driving directions (Mapbox) and lay them into a trip's stop_times.
capabilities.whenToUse: You need realistic times but don't have a timetable to copy.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Timetable tab → a trip's actions menu → 'Estimate times…'.
capabilities.limitations: Road-driving estimate; review before publishing.

capabilities.name: Interpolate stop times
capabilities.purpose: Fill in intermediate stop_times evenly between the timed stops you've entered.
capabilities.whenToUse: You've set a few key times and want the rest filled in.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Timetable tab → a trip's actions menu → 'Interpolate stop times'.

capabilities.name: Convert frequencies to individual trips
capabilities.purpose: Materialize a frequency/headway template into real, individual trips (and drop the frequencies row).
capabilities.whenToUse: You authored headway service but need explicit per-trip times.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Timetable tab → a trip's actions menu → 'Convert to trips…' (also offered from the Blocks Gantt).

capabilities.name: Quick Block (auto-blocking)
capabilities.purpose: Automatically group trips into vehicle blocks with a greedy heuristic, then review on the Gantt.
capabilities.whenToUse: You want a first-pass block assignment instead of assigning block_id by hand.
capabilities.clickPath: Left rail → Blocks → the Gantt → '⚡ Quick Block'.
capabilities.limitations: Heuristic first pass; review the result for overlaps.

capabilities.name: Continuous pickup/drop-off (flag-stop service)
capabilities.purpose: Model hail-and-ride / flag-stop boarding between stops via continuous_pickup / continuous_drop_off on routes and stop_times. This is how you build a circulator with continuous pickup and drop-off.
capabilities.whenToUse: On-route boarding/alighting between defined stops (circulators, rural flag-stop service).
capabilities.clickPath: Enable 'Flag-stop service (continuous pickup/drop-off)' in Settings, then set route-level values in the Route editor and per-stop ⚑ overrides in the timetable column header.
capabilities.limitations: Off by default; the controls only appear once the Flag-stop feature is enabled in Settings (or the imported feed already uses continuous_pickup/continuous_drop_off).

capabilities.name: Import & export feeds
capabilities.purpose: Start from an existing feed (upload a .zip, pull from your saved feeds, or import from the Mobility Database) and export a spec-clean GTFS .zip.
capabilities.whenToUse: Bringing a feed in to edit, or getting a finished .zip out.
capabilities.clickPath: Top bar → Import (Upload / My Feeds / Mobility Database) and Export.

capabilities.name: GeoJSON export
capabilities.purpose: Export route shapes and stops as a GeoJSON FeatureCollection for QGIS/ArcGIS/Mapbox and other GIS tools.
capabilities.whenToUse: You need the feed's geometry in a GIS, not as GTFS.
capabilities.clickPath: Top bar → Export → choose GeoJSON.
capabilities.limitations: Exports geometry (shapes + stops), not the full schedule.

capabilities.name: Draft & review links
capabilities.purpose: Share a link to a working feed so stakeholders can review it before you publish.
capabilities.whenToUse: Getting sign-off on a feed before it goes live.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Publish tab → draft/review links.
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey draft_links).

capabilities.name: Public feed catalog (discoverability)
capabilities.purpose: Opt a published feed into the public GTFS·X catalog (feeds.gtfsx.com/catalog.json) so open-transit aggregators can discover it.
capabilities.whenToUse: Making a hosted feed publicly discoverable to catalogs like the Mobility Database.
capabilities.clickPath: Bottom panel → Publish tab → opt the feed into the public catalog (feed must be published).
capabilities.limitations: Discovery is via the catalog (aggregators pull from it) — GTFS·X does not push directly to the Mobility Database API. You can also submit your feeds.gtfsx.com URL to the Mobility Database yourself via its contribution form.

capabilities.name: Rider propensity heatmap (demand dots)
capabilities.purpose: Layer ride-likelihood density onto the map to prioritize where to invest service.
capabilities.whenToUse: Deciding where demand is highest.
capabilities.clickPath: Enable the propensity/demand-dots map layer from the map layer controls.
capabilities.limitations: A map overlay, not an editable panel.

capabilities.name: Organizations & team workspaces
capabilities.purpose: Share feeds inside an organization and collaborate with teammates.
capabilities.whenToUse: Working with a team on the same feeds.
capabilities.clickPath: Account → Organizations.
capabilities.limitations: Requires the Planner plan (FeatureKey org_workspace). Feeds sync per account/org but there is no live simultaneous multi-user co-editing.

capabilities.name: Accounts & cloud sync
capabilities.purpose: Sign in to save feeds to your account (R2-backed) and sync across devices; anonymous editing works without an account too.
capabilities.whenToUse: Saving feeds to the cloud or moving between devices.
capabilities.clickPath: Sign in from the top bar; feeds save to your account automatically.

capabilities.name: Deep-link imports
capabilities.purpose: Open GTFS·X on a specific feed via a shareable deep link (e.g. from the Mobility Database).
capabilities.whenToUse: Jumping straight into editing a catalog feed.
capabilities.clickPath: Follow a /import deep link, or the Import dialog's Mobility Database tab.

notSupported.ask: Reroute a route by dragging its existing line on the map
notSupported.reason: Routes are edited by drawing or trimming their shape geometry, not by grabbing an existing route line and dragging it to a new path.
notSupported.workaround: Open the route's Shapes tab and edit/trim the shape, or redraw the line (snap-to-road will follow the streets).

notSupported.ask: Translate stop/route names into other languages (translations.txt)
notSupported.reason: There is no in-app translations editor; GTFS·X doesn't author translations.txt.
notSupported.workaround: Export the feed and add a translations.txt by hand; keep it out of a re-import so it isn't dropped.

notSupported.ask: Ferry / boat-specific route tooling (water routing)
notSupported.reason: There are no ferry-specific helpers or on-water route drawing; the map-matching/road tools assume streets.
notSupported.workaround: Create the route, set its route_type to Ferry (4) in the Route editor, and draw the shape by hand (keep the raw drawn line rather than snapping to roads).

notSupported.ask: Generate a live GTFS-Realtime TripUpdates or VehiclePositions feed
notSupported.reason: Only GTFS-Realtime Service Alerts are authored in-app; live TripUpdates and VehiclePositions are not produced by GTFS·X.
notSupported.workaround: Publish TripUpdates/VehiclePositions from your AVL/ITS/real-time system; use GTFS·X for the schedule and Service Alerts.

notSupported.ask: Fully author the whole Fares v2 model in-app (products, leg/transfer rules, rider categories)
notSupported.reason: Fares v2 authoring in-app currently ships the Areas editor (areas.txt + stop_areas.txt); the rest of the v2 model isn't fully editable yet.
notSupported.workaround: Import a feed that already carries those v2 files (they're preserved on export), or hand-edit after export.

notSupported.ask: Real-time collaborative editing with multiple people in the same feed at once
notSupported.reason: Feeds sync to your account/org, but there's no Google-Docs-style simultaneous multi-user co-editing — one editor at a time per feed.
notSupported.workaround: Use an org workspace to share the feed and coordinate who edits when.

notSupported.ask: Find-and-replace or bulk-rename across the whole feed (e.g. rename every stop matching a pattern)
notSupported.reason: There's no global find-and-replace tool; edits are made per entity (stop, route, trip) in their panels.
notSupported.workaround: Export the feed, do the bulk edit in the CSV (stops.txt, routes.txt, …), and re-import.

notSupported.ask: Full edit history / unlimited undo across sessions
notSupported.reason: Undo/redo covers the current editing session only — there's no persistent, unlimited edit history you can scrub back through after a reload.
notSupported.workaround: Use named snapshots (Planner plan) to checkpoint the feed and restore any prior saved state.

notSupported.ask: Export to NeTEx, TransXChange, or another transit-data format
notSupported.reason: GTFS·X exports GTFS (.zip) and GeoJSON only — it doesn't produce NeTEx, TransXChange, or other transit exchange formats.
notSupported.workaround: Export GTFS and convert to the target format with an external converter.

notSupported.ask: Plan a rider trip / journey (door-to-door itinerary) inside GTFS·X
notSupported.reason: GTFS·X is an editor and analysis tool, not a trip planner — it won't compute specific rider itineraries. (Access isochrones show the reachable area from a point, not a turn-by-turn journey.)
notSupported.workaround: Publish the feed and let a trip planner that consumes GTFS (Google Maps, Transit app, OpenTripPlanner) route riders.

notSupported.ask: Import running times from AVL / APC / GPS data to build the schedule automatically
notSupported.reason: GTFS·X doesn't ingest AVL/APC or GPS data; stop times are entered by hand or estimated from driving directions.
notSupported.workaround: Use 'Estimate travel times' (Mapbox driving estimate) as a starting point, then adjust the times in the timetable.

notSupported.ask: Offline maps or a custom basemap
notSupported.reason: The map renders on Mapbox's basemap and needs a connection; there's no offline mode or custom/self-hosted basemap.

notSupported.ask: Keep a custom (non-spec) column or an extra GTFS file through import/export
notSupported.reason: Unknown columns and unknown files are dropped on import — they aren't preserved or round-tripped (the one named exception is an agency's NTD / external ID).
notSupported.workaround: Re-add the custom column/file to the exported .zip after each export.

notSupported.ask: Author attributions.txt (data-source credit)
notSupported.reason: attributions.txt isn't modeled in GTFS·X — it's never read on import or written on export.
notSupported.workaround: Add attributions.txt to the exported .zip by hand (it won't survive a re-import).

notSupported.ask: Push my feed to the Mobility Database from inside GTFS·X
notSupported.reason: The Mobility Database API rejects third-party pushes, so there's no working in-product push — the button/path is a stub.
notSupported.workaround: Opt your published feed into the public GTFS·X catalog (feeds.gtfsx.com/catalog.json) that aggregators pull from, and/or submit your feeds.gtfsx.com URL to the Mobility Database yourself via its contribution form.

notSupported.ask: Submit my feed to TransitLand (transit.land) from GTFS·X
notSupported.reason: The in-product TransitLand submission is stubbed — it marks the feed pending for manual review rather than making a real API call.
notSupported.workaround: Submit your feeds.gtfsx.com URL to transit.land directly through its own contribution flow.

notSupported.ask: Publish my feed at my own domain instead of feeds.gtfsx.com/<slug>
notSupported.reason: Custom publishing domains (per-tenant TLS) are deferred — hosted feeds live under feeds.gtfsx.com.
notSupported.workaround: Point your own domain at the feeds.gtfsx.com URL with a 301 redirect.

notSupported.ask: Assign drivers/operators to blocks or build a duty roster (runcutting)
notSupported.reason: GTFS·X does vehicle blocking, not crew scheduling/runcutting — that's out of scope.
notSupported.workaround: Export the blocks and do runcutting in dedicated scheduling software.

notSupported.ask: Auto-generate or auto-push service alerts (e.g. from live disruptions)
notSupported.reason: Service Alerts are authored manually — there's no automatic alert generation or rider push from a live data source.
notSupported.workaround: Write the alert by hand in the Service Alerts panel.

notSupported.ask: Add a labeled landmark / point-of-interest marker (hospital, mall) to the map
notSupported.reason: There's no generic POI or custom-marker layer — the only points you place are transit stops, which export to stops.txt.

notSupported.ask: Import a GeoJSON boundary directly as a GTFS-Flex zone
notSupported.reason: Flex zones are drawn on the map or arrive inside an imported GTFS feed — there's no standalone GeoJSON-to-zone import.
notSupported.workaround: Wrap the GeoJSON as locations.geojson in a minimal GTFS .zip and import that.
